mnemonics: SYS: 'Shell Your String' - The system function passes a string command directly to the system's default shell for execution.
keyPoints: The system() function is declared in the stdlib.h header file
It takes a single string argument containing the shell command to execute
It returns the exit status of the executed command, or -1 on error
commonMistakes: Forgetting that system() uses /bin/sh, which may behave differently than your login shell (e.g., bash)
Not properly checking the return value to detect execution failures
Using system() with unsanitized user input, creating security vulnerabilities
